content:
## What the CX Page Is

The **CX page** (short for **Commissioning page**) is a site page in this ledger tool that tracks **commissioning readiness** for every installed equipment system across the project.

## What It Contains

Each row is a **test item** with these fields:

| Field | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Test ID** (e.g. `CX-212200-01-L1`) | Unique test identifier = spec-section + equipment-index + test-level |
| **Level** (L1 / L2 / L3) | Commissioning test tier (factory witness → site integration → integrated system) |
| **System** (e.g. `21 22 00`) | MasterFormat spec section |
| **Spec Clause** (e.g. `21 22 00 Part 3.4.1.A`) | Exact acceptance criteria from specification |
| **Status** | Currently all **PENDING** — no test has been executed yet |
| **Ledger Status** | Relationship to current submittal ledger state |
| **Open NCRs on Equipment** | Count of unresolved non-conformances on that equipment (blocking readiness) |
| **Ready** | `true` / `false` — overall readiness gate for that test |

## What It Is Used For

- **Gating** — determines whether each piece of equipment is actually ready for commissioning. If any open NCRs exist or the spec clause hasn't been satisfied, `ready = false`.
- **Test planning** — provides the full inventory of required tests per equipment per spec section, mapped to three levels:
  - **L1** – Factory acceptance / witness test
  - **L2** – Site installation & integration test
  - **L3** – End-to-end integrated system test
- **Linking to the queue** — every deviation package in the queue (`SUB-212200-01-R0`, etc.) has a `cx_tests` array that connects back to these test IDs, so schedule/cost decisions account for commissioning impact.

## Significance

- **Directly blocks project closeout** — until a test's acceptance criteria are met and its `ready` flag flips to `true`, the system cannot be handed over.
- **Risk indicator** — the CX page instantly shows which equipment groups are not commissioning-ready due to open NCRs:
  - ⚠ **Not ready** (currently): All **21 22 00** (clean-agent fire suppression) tests have **5 open NCRs** each. All **23 81 23** (CRAH units) tests have **3 open NCRs**.
  - ✅ **Ready** (currently): **21 10 00** (VESDA/aspirating smoke detection) and **23 64 26** (chillers) have **zero open NCRs**.
- **Schedule governor** — the CX page data feeds directly into the **clock** and **blast** pages: a `ready = false` test tied to a critical-path activity means the project cannot achieve substantial completion.
reasoning_content: I now have a comprehensive understanding of what the CX page is. Let me synthesize the answer.
